patterns_recognized: Consecutive-day same-matchup trap: NYY@OAK fires PLATINUM for a 3rd straight day after Day-1 8-2 blowout (NYY) then Day-2 6-4 OAK rebound; OAK lineup (Kurtz 1.096 RHP OPS, Rooker) remains dangerous.
D-grade dual-opposing-signal (SP xERA + PQI both against pick) shows ~25-30% directional win rate in archive; MIL@HOU (-3 score) and KC@TEX (-2 score) match this archetype exactly today.
HARD CAP 1 (negative-edge GOLD) validated on every fire in the visible archive — market pricing authority holds even when model direction is correct; do NOT loosen.
F1_xera_gap asterisk (thin-sample SP) anchoring without F2/F3 support drove xERA-projection failures on 5/26 DET and 5/28 HOU; today LAA@TB and NYY@OAK both carry F1* asterisks.
fair_prob < 0.42 combined with edge > +18pp marks calibration breakdown: market is far more skeptical than model (MIL@HOU fair_prob=0.324, MIA@NYM fair_prob=0.277); not a contrarian opportunity.

TOR @ BAL.reasoning: BAL sits at D-grade (-1) with dual opposing signals: Spencer Miles holds 1.18 xERA edge over Bradish and PQI opposes BAL at Δ=-13.9. Archive hit rate for D-grade dual-opposing picks is ~25-30%. fair_prob and edge are both missing, adding model uncertainty. BvP for both lineups is mostly NO_DATA vs these SPs. No qualitative reason to override the D-grade SKIP; caps are correct.

SD @ WSH.reasoning: SD at 54.33% with strong pre-cap signals (PQI=-27.2, SP edge 2.00 favoring SD). HARD CAP 1 correctly fires at edge=-0.20pp — market already prices SD at 54.53%, leaving no model-over-market edge. Machado is OWNER vs Canning (15 PA, shrunk 0.813); WSH's Wood is small-sample elevated OPS but only 9 PA. Direction is SD but SKIP enforced correctly — no stake has value when we are below market conviction.

MIN @ PIT.reasoning: Edge=-26.93pp is the most extreme negative edge on today's slate — market prices PIT at 78.2% vs our 51.27%. Paying market odds on a team we rate at 51% is indefensible. HARD CAP 1 is essential here. BvP for both lineups is all NO_DATA vs Zebby Matthews / Braxton Ashcraft. The bullpen_fatigue flag adds further caution. SKIP enforced; no stake has positive EV.

BOS @ CLE.reasoning: BOS at 54.63% with F1_xera_gap=1.29* (thin-sample asterisk on Suarez). HARD CAP 1 fires at edge=-9.12pp; market prices BOS more favorably than we do. CLE's Jose Ramirez shows SMALL_SAMPLE shrunk 0.872 vs Suarez (6 PA) — soft positive for CLE. Yesterday BOS won 9-1; today's Bibee/Suarez rematch has different dynamics. SKIP is correct; no stake without positive model edge.

LAA @ TB.reasoning: TB at B- (score=1) but F1_xera_gap=1.96* is thin-sample (Kochanowicz, LAA). Edge=-14.94pp — market prices TB at 79.4% vs our 64.43%; staking TB has no value if market is more confident. Yesterday LAA demolished TB 14-3 in the same matchup; same teams on back-to-back days with sub-65% model probability and thin SP sample. acute_roster warning adds roster uncertainty. Downgrading to SKIP: no conviction basis given large negative edge and thin F1 signal.
lessons_applied: F1* thin-sample asterisk as anchor risk

ATL @ CIN.reasoning: ATL at 56.13% with the strongest PQI on today's slate (Delta=-47.9). Spencer Strider (ATL) vs thin-sample Nick Lodolo (CIN, min_pa=92). ATL lineup (Acuna, Harris, Olson, Albies) dominates CIN's top order. Edge=+1.88pp is just below the 4pp floor preventing stake. CONFIRM ATL directionally — the PQI signal is compelling and Strider vs thin-sample Lodolo is real — but SKIP tier is correct given edge outside valid range.

MIA @ NYM.reasoning: Market prices MIA at only 27.7% (fair_prob=0.277, below 0.42 floor; edge=+23.90pp in calibration caution band). Yesterday MIA lost 1-6 to NYM in the same matchup. NYM's Wood (9 PA, shrunk 0.913) and Abrams (8 PA, shrunk 0.911) show elevated small-sample BvP vs McLean. calibration_caution_18_25pp flag present. Market is deeply skeptical of MIA; consecutive-day loss pattern argues against model's direction. DOWNGRADE to SKIP.

DET @ CHW.reasoning: CHW at 55.5% with PQI mildly opposing (Delta=-4.0), pre-cap score=1 (B-) already low. Edge=-1.26pp is essentially zero — market and model agree. HARD CAP 10 correctly zeroes stake in the bottom bucket. CHW's Benintendi shows SMALL_SAMPLE shrunk 0.665 vs Montero — mild headwind. Yesterday CHW won 7-1 comfortably; today is different SP matchup. Caps are correct; no new qualitative information overrides.

MIL @ HOU.reasoning: Grade D (-3) with three stacked opposing signals: Misiorowski holds 3.52 xERA advantage, PQI=-53.5 (extreme — strongest PQI opposing any pick on slate), Stage 1/2 delta=0.3044 but hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0005 means no bullpen carry support for Stage-2 reversal. Market prices HOU at 32.37% vs our 51.19% (edge=+18.82pp, calibration_caution). All quality signals favor MIL. DOWNGRADE: D-grade dual-opposing with no bullpen carry is the weakest structural archetype in the archive.
lessons_applied: Stage 1/2 disagree with no bullpen carry

KC @ TEX.reasoning: TEX at 50.58% with D-grade (-2): both SP edge (Wacha better xERA than Leiter) and PQI (Delta=-9.0) oppose TEX. Edge=-0.59pp — market and model agree at near coin-flip. Yesterday TEX won 7-6 but today has different SP matchup and the dual-opposing-signal D-grade pattern (~25-30% accuracy) still applies. SKIP is correct; no qualitative case for overriding the D-grade on a 50.58% pick.

SF @ COL.reasoning: COL pre-cap score=5 (A) — strongest pre-cap signal on slate: F3, SP edge 0.94 (Robbie Ray veteran vs Tanner Gordon), PQI=+15.3. Coors Field run-inflation consistently favors home team. Edge=+2.96pp just below 4pp floor blocks stake; HARD CAP 10 also fires. Yesterday COL won 8-3 at home vs SF. acute_roster flag on COL adds caution but signals remain directionally strong. CONFIRM direction; stake blocked by edge outside range.
lessons_applied: pre-cap signal quality as directional anchor

NYY @ OAK.reasoning: THIRD CONSECUTIVE DAY of NYY@OAK: Day 1 NYY won 8-2 (blowout), Day 2 OAK rebounded 6-4 (PLATINUM trap validated). Today NYY fires PLATINUM again with F1_xera_gap=1.12* (thin-sample Jacob Lopez, OAK SP). Edge=-8.67pp — market prices NYY at 64.8% vs our 56.13%. OAK's Kurtz (1.096 RHP career OPS, 513 PA) and Rooker remain dangerous. HARD CAP 1 and HARD CAP 10 both zero the stake. DOWNGRADE: three-consecutive-day pattern plus OAK lineup quality warrants skepticism.

ARI @ SEA.reasoning: ARI at 53.13% but facing Bryce Miller (SEA), who holds 6.10 xERA advantage over Merrill Kelly — largest SP-edge opposing the model pick on today's slate. PQI Delta=+60.5 for SEA (strongest PQI opposing pick on slate). Market prices ARI at 40.94% (fair_prob=0.409, below 0.42 floor). ARI lost 5-1 in SEA yesterday. F3+F5 fire for ARI (bullpen gap) but two overwhelming quality signals point to SEA. DOWNGRADE: SEA's directional case is stronger.

PHI @ LAD.reasoning: LAD at B- (score=1) with three F-signals firing (F1*+F2+F3: SP edge 0.56 for Yamamoto, xwOBA gap=0.036, F3=8879.3 — largest swing-take gap on slate). Andrew Painter (PHI) carries F1* thin-sample; Yamamoto is an established ace. PHI's top batters historically below-average vs Yamamoto: Schwarber shrunk 0.685, Harper shrunk 0.685, Bohm shrunk 0.655 (all SMALL_SAMPLE). Market even more bullish on LAD (72.3%) than model (63.83%). CONFIRM: strongest multi-signal stack on any actionable game today.

CHC @ STL.reasoning: Jordan Wicks (STL) has only 80 Statcast pitches — below the 100-pitch threshold for reliable xERA scoring. PENDING_SP_DATA protocol correct. On 5/26 this same cap prevented stake when PIT demolished CHC 12-1 after Wicks had 0 pitches. No directional call is possible without a scorable SP; zero-data SP threshold remains essential.
